data: Fashion Bawal Poore Saal — Summer Campaign

The "Fashion Bawal Poore Saal" campaign is built on the philosophy that "bawal" (disruption/impact) doesn't take a break just because the temperature rises; it simply gets hotter. For this summer edition, we are stripping away the "fancy" filters and leaning into a Raw, Editorial Street-Vibe that captures the relentless energy of kids and youngsters who own the streets regardless of the sun. The narrative is simple: while the world slows down in the heat, our audience ramps up the chaos, turning sweaty, sun-scorched days into a high-fashion statement.
Fashion Bawal Poore Saal — Summer Campaign — Tara Devi
A raw, editorial street-vibe summer campaign capturing the relentless energy of kids and youngsters who own the streets, turning sun-scorched days into a high-fashion statement for Citykart.
Fashion Bawal Poore Saal, Summer Campaign, Creative Direction, Editorial Street-Vibe, Trend Forecasting, Visual Storytelling, Citykart, Tara Devi

For this summer edition, we are stripping away the "fancy" filters and leaning into a Raw, Editorial Street-Vibe that captures the relentless energy of kids and youngsters who own the streets regardless of the sun.
The narrative is simple: while the world slows down in the heat, our audience ramps up the chaos, turning sweaty, sun-scorched days into a high-fashion statement.
A "luxury-to-mass" winter campaign bridging global aesthetics with high-volume social storytelling..
Winter Campaign: Global Aesthetic Scaling — Tara Devi
A luxury-to-mass winter campaign bridging global aesthetics with high-volume social storytelling, featuring 100+ creative assets and cinematic brand reels for Citykart.
Winter Campaign, Global Aesthetic Scaling, Luxury-to-Mass, Social Storytelling, Art Direction, Campaign Production, Citykart, Tara Devi
Conceptualized visual mood boards and storyboards, defining the aesthetic direction, lighting references, and color stories for the entire winter season.
Collaborated with Art Directors and Photographers to translate brand concepts into high-impact visual assets, ensuring every shot met the campaign's creative brief.
Curated and mapped 200+ outfits, managing the technical "flat lay" and "on-model" styling to ensure consistency across the digital lookbook.
Led model scouting and talent selection, personally vetting and casting talent that embodied the brand's winter persona.
Directed on-site location scouting, identifying and securing environments that aligned with the storyboard's atmospheric requirements.
Orchestrated the production of 100+ creative assets, overseeing the transition from raw footage to social-optimized reels with a focus on high-retention hooks.
Managed the end-to-end creative workflow, from initial outfit planning and artist briefings to final post-production and asset delivery.

Elevated the brand's perception in Tier 2, 3, and 4 markets by benchmarking art direction against global luxury standards, effectively bridging the gap between high-end international aesthetics and local accessibility.

Strategized the use of foreign talent and premium styling to offer a "window into luxury" for regional audiences, differentiating the brand from the utilitarian imagery typically used by competitors in these territories.
Produced 100+ creative assets to fuel an aggressive social calendar, ensuring the brand dominated the "share of voice" on platforms like Instagram and Facebook during the peak winter shopping window.
Designed a dual-track shooting workflow that enabled the simultaneous capture of cinematic brand reels and an editorial photoshoot, maximizing asset output without doubling production time.

Curated a specific "look and feel" that combined high-fashion foreign model casting with relatable winter styling, creating a unique "Global-Local" (Glocal) identity that felt both premium and reachable for regional consumers.
Cleverly utilized high-end art direction and foreign casting to "price up" the brand's perception, allowing a mid-market product to compete for the psychological mindshare of a luxury consumer.
Architected a high-gloss visual narrative that suggested an international budget while maintaining a lean, high-output production model—forcing competitors to overspend to match our visual standard.

“Dilon Ka Tyohar” is more than a tagline; it is our brand’s heartbeat. It stems from a simple truth: while clothes are draped over the body, they are always chosen by the heart. In this campaign, we redefine the act of shopping. It is no longer a transaction; it is the "First Celebration"—the joyous, expectant prelude to the festivities. We position our marketing designs as the threshold where the festival begins, and our films as the mirror reflecting a family’s shared dreams.
Dilon Ka Tyohar — Festive Campaign — Tara Devi

A festive campaign redefining shopping as the 'First Celebration' — the joyous prelude to festivities where clothes are chosen by the heart, featuring films, brand reels, and editorial designs for Citykart.
Dilon Ka Tyohar, Festive Campaign, Art Direction, Film Production, Creative Strategy, Visual Identity, Stakeholder Management, Citykart, Tara Devi

From immersive storefront window displays and dynamic auto-hood branding to 3D-effect hoardings, these works transform traditional advertising into an experiential journey.
The Summer Archive: A Debut Production — Tara Devi
A high-energy summer campaign built on DIY grit and creative problem-solving, transforming a green-screen studio into a vibrant, sun-soaked world on a modest budget for Citykart.
Summer Archive, Debut Production, Creative Direction, Green Screen, DIY Production, Compositing, Production Design, Citykart, Tara Devi

This curated showcase features high-impact marketing activations and innovative creative strategies designed to dominate the consumer's daily path. From immersive storefront window displays and dynamic auto-hood branding to 3D-effect hoardings, these works transform traditional advertising into an experiential journey. By reimagining every touchpoint—including culturally-rich newspaper campaigns and architectural-scale public installations—passive viewers are turned into active brand ambassadors. This collection represents the intersection of bold art direction and measurable retail growth, successfully driving massive footfall and digital engagement across diverse urban markets.

The "Local Pride" - We saw the internet go crazy over the bold Zomato-Blinkit billboards and thought: “Why should digital have all the fun?” We hijacked that viral energy and brought it straight to the heart of the streets.

We've officially ditched traditional, "model-heavy" posters for a complete offline branding revolution. By replacing boring visuals with Bold, Quirky, and Meme-Inspired designs, we've turned our store windows and Autohood Innovations into moving conversation starters. No photos, just pure personality and high-voltage fashion

The "Classroom Canvas" display focuses on the nostalgic and playful side of education. By using a large-scale chalkboard as the primary "Offer Board," the design immediately communicates a "Back-to-School" message. The composition uses oversized 2D elements like the pencil and backpack to create a sense of wonder, making the school season feel like an adventure rather than a chore. The neutral grey background allows the bright yellow and red accents to pop, ensuring that the 40% OFF messaging is the first thing a customer notices from a distance.

Dilon Ka Tyohar" - The Heart of the Festival 
This window is a cultural centerpiece designed specifically for the festive season (Durga Puja). It shifts the brand tone from functional to emotional by using traditional motifs like the Kash Phool (wild grass), the Shankha (conch shell), and the Maa Durga mask. The deep crimson backdrop creates a premium, celebratory atmosphere that honors local traditions. By positioning the mannequins under a decorative white archway, the display mimics a Pandal (traditional marquee), successfully inviting customers to visualize themselves in new festive attire for the "Festival of the Heart".

"Masti Ki Pathshala" – Fun at School 
The third display is a dynamic and geometric take on the academic theme, utilizing the concept of "The School of Fun." Unlike the first window, this one uses vibrant, oversized stationery like the 3D-effect yellow set-square and red ruler to frame the merchandise. The central "Open Book" serves as both a thematic element and a promotional sign, creating a literal story for the consumer to read. The use of a "Crayon" style background adds a layer of creativity and energy, specifically targeting younger children and encouraging a joyful approach to the upcoming school year.

Our goal was to evolve the traditional "logo wall" into an architectural brand experience. By utilizing a recessed archway and integrated LED halo lighting, we created a high-contrast, professional "stage" that invites the customer to step into the brand's world.

The play between the textured red exterior and high-gloss interior ensures the installation pops against neutral retail environments, while the strategic "I ❤️ Citykart" placement guarantees the brand is the hero of every social media share. It’s not just a backdrop; it’s a physical-to-digital bridge designed to turn foot traffic into brand advocacy.

The Citykart Lucknow campaign moved beyond traditional signage to execute a series of innovative hoardings designed to stop traffic. The project focused on high-impact visual storytelling through two distinct approaches: a custom 2D-cutout illustration that breaks the billboard’s physical boundary to create a 3D pop effect, and a hyper-realistic lifestyle grid that showcases the product range with premium clarity.

By blending localized pop-culture messaging with modern, architectural layouts, these hoardings successfully cut through the urban clutter. This execution was a bold visual statement that established Citykart as a contemporary, relatable brand in the heart of the city through a creative, boundary-pushing format.

To disrupt the visual landscape in smaller city markets, this initiative reimagined the traditional storefront as an immersive brand landmark. By transforming the iconic shopping bag into a massive, architectural gateway directly in front of the store, the project moved beyond static signage to create a high-impact, "Instagrammable" mural experience. This clever shift toward experiential urban interventions turned the store entrance into a magnetic public focal point, generating significant buzz and social sharing. Executed across multiple locations, these installations successfully delivered a unique brand experience that boosted foot traffic by 40%, establishing a contemporary and relatable presence right at the customer's point of entry

This series of high-impact newspaper advertisements reimagined the retail circular as a vibrant cultural touchpoint, blending dense product catalogs with rich seasonal storytelling. By utilizing a "Grid-to-Glow" design strategy for campaigns like Lagan Utsav and Bada Sasta Hai, the layouts maintain high information density while using festive iconography to create an immediate emotional connection.
